examples.title: Memory Read Operation
examples.description: Demonstrates the sequence of operations for reading data from memory into a CPU register
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the hardware-level sequence for a memory read operation. The CPU places the address in MAR, signals a read, waits for memory to respond, and transfers the data to the destination register.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) - Uses constant number of registers

examples.title: PUSH and POP Operations
examples.description: Demonstrates stack PUSH and POP operations using memory
examples.explanation: PUSH decrements the stack pointer and stores data at the new top. POP retrieves data from the top and increments the stack pointer. This LIFO structure is essential for function call management.

examples.title: Little-Endian to Big-Endian Conversion
examples.description: Demonstrates byte ordering conversion between little-endian and big-endian representations
examples.code:
def swap_endian_32(value):
    """
    Convert 32-bit value between little-endian and big-endian.
    Example: 0x12345678 -> 0x78563412
    """
    # Extract 4 bytes
    byte0 = (value >> 0) & 0xFF   # Least significant byte
    byte1 = (value >> 8) & 0xFF
    byte2 = (value >> 16) & 0xFF
    byte3 = (value >> 24) & 0xFF   # Most significant byte
    
    # Reconstruct in reverse order (big-endian)
    big_endian = (byte0 << 24) | (byte1 << 16) | (byte2 << 8) | byte3
    
    return big_endian

# Example usage
original = 0x12345678
converted = swap_endian_32(original)
print(f"Original: 0x{original:08X}")
print(f"Converted: 0x{converted:08X}")
print(f"Little-endian bytes: {original.to_bytes(4, 'little').hex()}")
print(f"Big-endian bytes: {original.to_bytes(4, 'big').hex()}")
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates how to convert between little-endian and big-endian byte ordering. In little-endian, the least significant byte is stored at the lowest address. The conversion reverses the byte order.

examples.title: Memory Access Simulation
examples.description: Simulates memory read and write operations with timing considerations
examples.explanation: This C program simulates basic memory read and write operations. It demonstrates byte-addressable memory where each address holds one byte, and how multi-byte values are stored (using little-endian in this example).
